refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 second).

candidate_dps: Incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data to minimize latency
Batch update mechanism that processes full worksite model at fixed intervals
Event-driven update mechanism triggered by detected changes in the worksite
Parallel processing of update tasks to meet timeliness requirements
Prioritization of critical update elements to ensure timely operational use
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data to minimize latency

potential_sources: Dependency on upstream data change detection accuracy
Integration with safety plan and operational policy enforcement modules
coupling_check.notes: The incremental update mechanism logically isolates update processing to changed data, supporting independence from full model processing. Coupling risks mainly arise from dependencies on accurate change detection and integration with safety and policy modules, which should be managed via clear interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and timely detection of changes in the worksite model is feasible
Incremental updates can be processed within the 1.0 s latency target under expected load
Safety plan and operational policies do not impose constraints that force full model updates
Potential complexity in managing incremental state consistency
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces for change detection and safety/policy enforcement to further reduce coupling and clarify responsibilities.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Software module responsible for data processing and update mechanisms within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives data updates and sends processed incremental updates

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates incremental updates to other system components

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the incremental update mechanism logically fits within the data processing responsibilities of NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion since updates are processed internally and communicated via defined interfaces.
Lower containment as Vehicle module focuses on physical control and state; incremental update logic would be partially outside its core scope, causing diffusion into other modules.
Teleoperation mainly handles remote control commands; incremental data updates are peripheral, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; incremental update mechanism is unrelated, resulting in very poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental update mechanism is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, which is responsible for data processing and system updates, ensuring high cohesion and minimal diffusion across the system.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if incremental updates affect vehicle state directly, requiring careful interface design.
If incremental updates require real-time safety checks, coordination with Safety Services may increase coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of an incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within customer-required timeliness by minimizing latency. This logical solution concept aligns well with the functional requirement and supports meeting the acceptance criteria of update latency p95 ≤ 1.0 s.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if incremental updates affect vehicle state directly, requiring careful interface design.
If incremental updates require real-time safety checks, coordination with Safety Services may increase coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment score (8) for the incremental update mechanism, with minimal diffusion (score 2). Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, indicating that the DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution IM boundary, minimizing diffusion risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring update latency metrics (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) on the NGHE Solution module during operational scenarios, and validating that processed incremental updates meet customer timeliness requirements.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate NGHE Solution module clearly owns the incremental update mechanism, with well-defined external interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) supporting data input and output, ensuring clear allocation of responsibility for realization of the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle and Safety Services modules to manage potential overlap and coupling risks.
Include verification steps to measure update latency within the NGHE Solution module to ensure compliance with acceptance criteria.
Monitor and review diffusion scores periodically to detect any creeping diffusion as system evolves, especially if incremental updates start affecting vehicle state or safety monitoring.

decision.justification: The current functional requirement (FR) of updating the worksite model within customer-required timeliness is adequately addressed by the selected design parameter (DP) of an incremental update mechanism, which has passed the FR->DP gate. The DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution implementation module (IM), with high cohesion and minimal diffusion, and the FR->IM gate confirms feasible verification and clear ownership. Given the high safety criticality, the main concerns relate to interface contracts and diffusion risk monitoring rather than further decomposition. Therefore, it is appropriate to stop decomposition at this level and focus on documenting interfaces, verification, and risk monitoring as recommended.

revise.why: Explicitly document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle and Safety Services modules to manage potential overlap and coupling risks, and include verification steps to measure update latency within the NGHE Solution module.

assumptions: Incremental update mechanism sufficiently addresses the timeliness FR.
NGHE Solution module ownership is clear and stable.
Interface contracts can effectively manage diffusion risks.
open_risks: Potential overlap and coupling with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.
Creeping diffusion if incremental updates affect vehicle state or safety monitoring.
Verification must confirm update latency meets acceptance criteria under operational conditions.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) from NGHE Solution module during operation.
Periodic diffusion score assessments to detect changes over time.
